publicationstmt.publisher: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday January 19, 2024 at 03:00

did.unittitle: Michael L. Charney papers

corpname.part: Medical Historical Library, Cushing/Whitney Medical Library

did.abstract: Michael L. Charney (1946 - ) is a psychiatrist and life-long public health and social rights activist. The collection documents Charney's student activism while attending the Yale School of Medicine from 1968-1972. Materials document his involvement with student protests at Yale and the Yale School of Medicine; work as editor of the Yale Medical Center Newsletter; involvement with student strikes and the creation of The Organizer's Manual; participation in health activism and social justice groups including the Medical Committee for Human Rights, Nader's Raiders Occupational Health and Safety Project, and Student Health Organizations; support of Vietnam War protests and his application for conscientious objector status to the draft; and involvement with community activists and protests in New Haven, including the Black Panther Party May Day protest. The collection also includes materials documenting Charney witnessing and reporting arson of a student protest structure on the Beinecke plaza in 1988. Materials include correspondence, ephemera, magazine articles and newspaper clippings, newsletters, notebooks, organizational records, pamphlets, posters, and publications.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Michael L. Charney (Yale School of Medicine class of 1972), 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection has been arranged into eight series: Series I. Medical activism, Series II. New Haven community activism, Series III. Organizer's Manual, Series IV. Political parties and elections, Series V. Vietnam War, Series VI. Yale, Series VII. Leftist literature, Series VIII. Articles about the 1960s, social movements, and protests.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Michael L. Charney (1946 - ) is a psychiatrist with a life-long commitment to activism. Charney graduated from Yale College in 1968, then attended Yale School of Medicine, completing an MD with a specialization in psychiatry in 1972. He completed an internship at Cambridge Hospital, residency at Harvard Medical School's Brigham and Women's Hospital, and a fellowship in child and adolescent psychiatry at Massachusetts General Hospital before opening a private practice in 1978.

During his time at Yale Charney was involved in activism and protests related to medical care, public health, occupational safety, the Vietnam War, racism and community planning, politics, and governance of universities. His activism continued after completing his MD and has included initiatives to reduce the proliferation of nuclear weapons, enact seatbelt laws, and advocate for environmental protection.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Michael L. Charney papers. Historical Library, Harvey Cushing / John Hay Whitney Medical Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection documents Michael L. Charney's student activism while attending the Yale School of Medicine from 1968-1972 including his work as editor of the Yale Medical Center Newsletter , a student activism newsletter for the Yale School of Medicine; his work for a student strike and the creation of The Organizer's Manual ; and his involvement with health activism and social justice movements including the Medical Committee for Human Rights, Nader's Raiders, the Vietnam War protests, women's liberation movement, and the Black Panthers. The collection also includes materials documenting Charney witnessing and reporting arson of a student protest structure on the Beinecke plaza in 1988. Materials include newsletters, pamphlets, newspaper clippings, documents, correspondence, ephemera, and posters.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has been transferred to Yale University for unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by Michael L. Charney. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.
